summary: Fixed Stripe lazy-init race condition in adminRoutes.js by consolidating two mutable variables (_stripe, _stripeKey) into a single _stripeCache={key,client} object

commits_made: 5c45df5 - fix(adminRoutes): replace two-variable Stripe lazy init with single cache object
context_for_next_worker.approach_used: Consolidated let _stripe=null and let _stripeKey=null into a single let _stripeCache={key,client}|null object; the atomic single-variable update eliminates the two-mutable-state code smell while preserving the runtime key rotation feature (reads settings.json on each request).
context_for_next_worker.what_worked: aiDiscoveryService.js did NOT need changes — it uses instance-level this.openai=null in a class with a synchronous _initOpenAI() method (no async yield, no race condition).
context_for_next_worker.what_didnt: The audit description was slightly outdated — the existing code already had a two-variable check (_stripe + _stripeKey) rather than the simpler null check described. The fix preserved the settings.json runtime key update feature.
context_for_next_worker.next_recommended_step: Check .overnight/active/ for remaining pending tasks. Active tasks include 002-P2-add-structured-logging.md and 003-P2-add-checkout-cryptocheckout-tests.md.
